info.name: Grobase — Offer capability proof (live stack)
info.description: Proves every Grobase OFFER capability works against the LIVE mini-baas stack through Kong (http://127.0.0.1:8002), the control plane (http://127.0.0.1:3022), and the Rust data plane. Each request carries pm.test assertions that check BOTH status AND body shape (never vacuous). Idempotent / re-runnable: a unique tenant slug is minted per run from {{$timestamp}}. SAFE on the live stack: it only CREATES a throwaway test tenant; it never deletes or mutates other tenants. Folders mirror the real verify gates: 10 Provision = scripts/verify/lib-live-tenant.sh; 30/40 Data+Query = m22/m26; 50 Aggregate = m26; 60 Transactions = openapi /query/v1/txn TxnRequest; 80 Storage = m55; 90 Functions = m56; 95 Self-service = m83 (accepts gated 404).

item.name: 00 Health
item.description: Liveness of all three planes. auth-gateway has no /health, so we probe a real auth endpoint (/auth/v1/health via Kong, which 200s with apikey). Kong itself is proven live by the data-plane and auth probes traversing it.

exec: // Traverses Kong key-auth → gotrue. A 200 proves Kong routes are live AND
// the gateway accepts the anon apikey. auth-gateway has no /health route,
// so this gotrue health route is the real liveness signal per CLAUDE.md.
pm.test('Kong → auth health is up (200)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code, 'status').to.be.oneOf([200, 201]));
// The realtime publish endpoint (/v1/publish) is INTERNAL-only (query-router →
// surface is the WebSocket subscribe at /realtime/v1/ws. A plain GET on the
// upstream /ws returns 400 'Connection header did not include upgrade' = the
// realtime server is alive and rejecting non-WS traffic (NOT a 404/5xx).
// Mirrors the realtime route in docker/services/kong/conf/kong.yml.
pm.test('realtime WS endpoint is reachable (400 upgrade-required, not 5xx)', () => {

item.name: 10 Provision
item.description: Mints a throwaway tenant + a postgresql mount + an mbk_ API key via the service-token admin API. Mirrors scripts/verify/lib-live-tenant.sh exactly: POST /v1/tenants → PATCH plan=enterprise (so all engines/ops are usable under PACKAGE_ENFORCEMENT) → POST /v1/tenants/{id}/keys → POST {Kong}/admin/v1/databases. Saves apiKey + dbId + slug into the environment for the data folders. The pre-request script derives a unique slug from {{$timestamp}} so re-runs never collide.

exec: // Idempotent / re-runnable: a fresh slug per run. tenant-control's slug
// regex is ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]{1,62}$ so we keep it lowercase-alnum.
// lib-live-tenant.sh: 201 created OR 409 already-exists are both fine (idempotent).
// Mirrors lib-live-tenant.sh step 1b: put the probe on enterprise (→ max tier)
// so every engine + op is usable under PACKAGE_ENFORCEMENT. Best-effort:
// an older tenant-control without PATCH leaves the default tier (still fine
// for the pg-only data folders), so we accept 200 OR a clean 4xx, never 5xx.
pm.test('plan upgrade accepted or cleanly unsupported (not 5xx)', () => {

item.name: 20 Auth
item.description: gotrue Supabase-shaped surface through Kong: signup → password grant → get user. NOTE: this stack runs GOTRUE_MAILER_AUTOCONFIRM=false, so a fresh signup is NOT auto-confirmed and the password grant returns 400 email_not_confirmed instead of a session. The folder asserts the documented success-or-pending-confirmation contract (never vacuous): signup returns a user; the grant returns a session OR a clean email_not_confirmed; get user reflects whichever path occurred.

item.name: 30 Data CRUD
item.description: Full insert → list → get → update → delete on the provisioned pg mount via Kong: POST /query/v1/{dbId}/tables/{table} with op in the body (mirrors m22 / m26). Auth = apikey (anon Kong consumer) + X-Baas-Api-Key (the tenant mbk_ key the ApiKeyMiddleware exchanges via tenant-control). Owner-scoping is implicit: the row is stamped with this key's principal, and the round-trip values prove it.

exec: // Unique per run so re-runs never collide on a pre-existing table.
// schema_ddl create_table is a pro+/max capability (m22). owner_id is auto-
// appended by the platform write path; we declare id+name+n.

item.name: 40 Query
item.description: Filter + projection + sort, the read-shaping capability every tier carries. Seeds three rows then proves: a filtered list returns only the matching rows, projection narrows the columns, and sort orders the result. Bodies mirror m26's list shapes.

exec: // op=batch is a pro+ capability (atomic on pg). Seeds three rows in one shot.
const rows = j.data || j.rows || j.results || (Array.isArray(j) ? j : []);
pm.test('filter honored: exactly the two name=gamma rows', () => pm.expect(rows.length).to.eql(2));
pm.test('every returned row is name=gamma', () => rows.forEach(r => pm.expect(r.name).to.eql('gamma')));
pm.test('sort honored: n ascending (q1.n=10 before q3.n=20)', () => { pm.expect(rows[0].id).to.eql('q1'); pm.expect(rows[1].id).to.eql('q3'); });

item.name: 50 Aggregate
item.description: op=aggregate — the essential+ capability (count / sum / group_by). Mirrors m26: count returns the row total, sum returns the correct number (tolerant of pg bigint number vs mysql DECIMAL string), and a group_by on name yields one group per distinct name. Reuses the q1/q2/q3 rows seeded in folder 40 (gamma×2, delta×1; n = 10+30+20 = 60).

exec: // hasnum-style: number OR string (mysql DECIMAL comes back as text), like m26.
const rows = j.data || j.rows || j.results || (Array.isArray(j) ? j : []);

item.name: 60 Transactions
item.description: POST /query/v1/txn — the pro+ multi-op atomic-write capability. Body = the openapi TxnRequest schema (databaseId + operations[]{op,resource,data,filter}). Proves two inserts commit atomically, then a follow-up aggregate confirms both rows landed. Auth = apikey + X-Baas-Api-Key (same posture as /query/v1).

exec: // The txn endpoint reports a committed/atomic outcome; assert it is not an error
// envelope and that it acknowledges the two operations.
pm.test('txn response is not an error envelope', () => {

item.name: Verify both txn rows landed — op=aggregate
70 Realtime
item.description: The realtime add-on (pro/max). The PUBLIC surface is the WebSocket subscribe at /realtime/v1/ws (in-band JWT AUTH then SUBSCRIBE); publish (/v1/publish) is INTERNAL-only — query-router posts row_changed to http://realtime:4000/v1/publish in-network (see realtime-publisher.service.ts + kong.yml). newman cannot drive a WebSocket, so this folder smoke-checks that the realtime plane is LIVE and the public route is wired: a plain GET on the WS endpoint must return 400 upgrade-required (server alive, rejecting non-WS) — never a 404 or 5xx. Full WS echo is covered by m22's WS subscriber.

exec: // /realtime/v1/ws is the dedicated, auth-skipping WS route (kong.yml realtime-ws).
// A plain HTTP GET cannot upgrade, so axum returns 400 'Connection header did
// not include upgrade' (or 426/101). 404/5xx would mean the plane is missing.
// Publish is INTERNAL-only by design: the public Kong realtime route maps
// to /ws/publish, which the realtime server does NOT mount publicly. The
// documented, secure outcome is therefore a 4xx (not exposed) — NOT a 5xx,
// and NOT a 200 (a public 200 would mean an unauthenticated publish hole).
pm.test('public publish is not exposed as an open 200 (4xx, not 5xx)', () => {

item.name: 80 Storage
item.description: Storage add-on (max). Mirrors m55: createBucket (POST /storage/v1/bucket/{name}) → upload (PUT /storage/v1/object/{bucket}/{key}) → list (GET /storage/v1/list/{bucket}). Storage requires a VERIFIED USER identity (JWT sub), NOT a tenant API key — Kong's pre-function decodes the JWT sub into X-User-Id (the owner key). The pre-request script mints an HS256 JWT (iss=supabase) using {{jwtSecret}} so the run is self-contained; an anon-only upload is asserted 401.

exec: // Mint an HS256 JWT (iss=supabase, sub=storage owner) like m55's mint_jwt.
pm.test.skip('jwtSecret not injected — storage skipped (runner discovers it from mini-baas-gotrue)');
pm.test('response names the bucket + created flag', () => {

item.name: 90 Functions
item.description: Functions add-on (max). Mirrors m56: deploy (POST /functions/v1) → list (GET /functions/v1) → invoke (POST /functions/v1/{name}/invoke). Like storage, functions namespace per VERIFIED USER (JWT sub), reusing {{storageJwt}} minted in folder 80. Asserts the deployed echo function actually runs in the Deno worker and returns the handler body; anon-only deploy is 401.

exec: pm.test('handler ran in the Deno worker (ok:true)', () => pm.expect(s).to.include('"ok":true'));

item.name: 95 Self-service
item.description: Track-B tenant self-service (/v1/tenants/me*, flag TENANT_SELFSERVE_ENABLED). Auth = X-API-Key (the mbk_ tenant key) on the CONTROL plane — NOT the data-plane X-Baas-Api-Key. Per m83 the surface resolves the caller to its OWN tenant (no path id) when ON, and is byte-parity 404/401 when OFF. This folder asserts works-OR-cleanly-disabled: GET /v1/tenants/me returns 200 with the caller's tenant, OR a gated 404/401/501 — it does NOT hard-fail on a gated route, so the baseline (flag OFF) stays green.

exec: // m83: ON → 200 resolving to the caller's own tenant; OFF → 401/404 (route not
// mounted / falls to the {id} wildcard). Accept the small allowed set; never 5xx.
pm.test('self-service works OR is cleanly disabled (200 / 401 / 404 / 501)', () => {
pm.test('200 resolves to THIS tenant (our slug) — no path id, no cross-tenant', () => {

// same gated set. Never a 5xx — that would be a real defect, not a flag.
